summary:
Nintedanib is FDA-approved for treating systemic sclerosis-associated interstitial lung disease and demonstrates efficacy in slowing the decline of pulmonary function, particularly forced vital capacity. It is recommended by leading respiratory guidelines due to proven benefits in delaying disease progression. 

Note that this summary does not include any information about off-label use or other conditions beyond what was asked.

The provided response correctly identifies the core clinical relevance—FDA approval and demonstrated benefit—in addressing lung involvement specific to systemic sclerosis without introducing irrelevant details like off-label applications or unrelated indications. The focus remains strictly within established treatment guidance related directly to scleroderma-related ILD.
